name: Treadmill Intervals
instructions: Warm up at an easy pace for 5-10 minutes.
Alternate hard work bouts (typically 30 seconds to 2 minutes at perceived effort 8-9/10, or 85-95% max HR) with easy recovery bouts (1-2 minutes at conversational pace).
Repeat for the prescribed number of work intervals.
Cool down at an easy pace for 3-5 minutes.

name: Treadmill Incline Walk
instructions: Set the treadmill incline between 6-15% based on prescription.
Walk at a brisk but sustainable pace (typically 3.0-3.8 mph) without holding the handrails.
Maintain steady breathing; the target is zone 2 cardio with added posterior-chain load from the incline.

name: Outdoor Walk
instructions: Walk outdoors at a brisk, conversational pace.
Maintain steady breathing and upright posture.
Used as low-impact zone 2 cardio or active recovery.

name: Outdoor Run
instructions: Warm up with 5 minutes of brisk walking or easy jogging.
Run outdoors at the prescribed pace or effort (zone 2, tempo, or intervals as indicated).
Cool down with 3-5 minutes of easy walking and light stretching.

name: Stationary Bike Intervals
instructions: Warm up at low resistance for 5 minutes.
Alternate hard intervals (30 seconds to 2 minutes at high resistance/cadence, perceived effort 8-9/10) with easy recovery spins.
Repeat for the prescribed number of intervals.
Cool down at low resistance for 3-5 minutes.

name: Rowing Machine Intervals
instructions: Warm up with 3-5 minutes of easy rowing.
Alternate hard rowing intervals (typically 250-500m or 1-2 min at strong pace) with light recovery rows.
Drive with the legs first, then hinge through the hips, then pull with the arms.
Cool down with 3-5 minutes of easy rowing.

name: Swimming
instructions: Swim continuous laps using freestyle or another sustainable stroke.
Maintain rhythmic bilateral breathing and a long, relaxed stroke.
Used as low-impact full-body cardio.

name: Swimming Intervals
instructions: Warm up with 200-400m of easy swimming.
Alternate hard intervals (typically 50-100m at fast pace) with rest at the wall or easy swim recovery.
Repeat for the prescribed number of intervals.
Cool down with 100-200m of easy swimming.

name: Incline Hike
instructions: Hike outdoors on inclined terrain at a steady, sustainable pace.
Use trekking poles if helpful; maintain even breathing.
Acts as low-impact zone 2 cardio with added posterior-chain and stabilizer work from uneven ground.

name: Jump Rope
instructions: Hold a jump rope with relaxed shoulders and elbows close to the ribs.
Skip rope using a small bounce off the balls of the feet, turning the rope with the wrists rather than the shoulders.
Used for warm-ups, conditioning intervals, or steady-state cardio.
